9597	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "According to
9598	% Cohomology, Lemma \ref{cohomology-lemma-describe-higher-direct-images}
9599	% the sheaf
9600	% $R^if_*\mathcal{F}$ is the sheaf associated to the presheaf
9601	% $V \mapsto H^i(f^{-1}(V), \mathcal{F}|_{f^{-1}(V)})$.
9602	% By assumption, whenever $V$ is affine we have that $f^{-1}(V)$ is
9603	% affine, see Morphisms, Definition \ref{morphisms-definition-affine}.
9604	% By Lemma \ref{lemma-quasi-coherent-affine-cohomology-zero} we conclude that
9605	% $H^i(f^{-1}(V), \mathcal{F}|_{f^{-1}(V)}) = 0$
9606	% whenever $V$ is affine. Since $S$ has a basis consisting of affine
9607	% opens we win."
9608	\begin{proof}
9609	  \uses{lem:affine_serre_vanishing, def:cech_nerve, lem:higher_direct_image_presheaf, lem:open_immersion_pushforward_comp}
9610	  A product of acyclic objects is acyclic, so it suffices to treat a single factor
9611	  \((j_s)_*(\mathcal{F}|_{U_s})\), where \(s = (i_0, \ldots, i_p)\), \(U_s =
9612	  U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\) is affine, and \(j_s : U_s \hookrightarrow X\) is the open
9613	  immersion. Acyclicity for \(f_*\) is a local question on \(S\): the derived
9614	  pushforward \(R^k f_*\, \mathcal{G}\) is the sheaf associated to the presheaf
9615	  \(V \mapsto H^k(f^{-1}(V), \mathcal{G}|_{f^{-1}(V)})\), so it vanishes for
9616	  \(k \geq 1\) iff that cohomology vanishes for all affine \(V \subseteq S\).
9617	  Restricting to such a \(V = \operatorname{Spec}(A)\), the base change \(f_V :
9618	  f^{-1}(V) \to V\) is again separated and quasi-compact over the separated base
9619	  \(V\), hence \(f^{-1}(V)\) is separated and the affine \(U_s\) meets it in the
9620	  affine open \(U_s \cap f^{-1}(V)\).
9621	
9622	  Now decompose the structure map \(g_s := f \circ j_s : U_s \to S\). First,
9623	  \(j_s\) is the inclusion of an affine open into the separated scheme \(X\), hence
9624	  an affine morphism, so the higher direct images \(R^q (j_s)_*\) vanish for
9625	  \(q \geq 1\). By Lemma~\ref{lem:open_immersion_pushforward_comp} part (2),
9626	  \(R^k f_*\bigl((j_s)_*(\mathcal{F}|_{U_s})\bigr) \cong
9627	  R^k (g_s)_*(\mathcal{F}|_{U_s})\). Second, working locally over an affine
9628	  \(V \subseteq S\) as above, \(g_s\) restricts to a morphism of affine schemes
9629	  \(U_s \cap f^{-1}(V) \to V\), which is an affine morphism; by the relative affine
9630	  vanishing \(R^k (g_s)_*(\mathcal{F}|_{U_s}) = 0\) for \(k \geq 1\). That relative
9631	  vanishing is itself a consequence of Serre vanishing
9632	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:affine_serre_vanishing}): \(R^k(g_s)_*\mathcal{G}\) is the
9633	  sheafification of \(V \mapsto H^k(g_s^{-1}(V), \mathcal{G})\), and for affine
9634	  \(V\) the preimage \(g_s^{-1}(V)\) is affine, on which higher cohomology of a
9635	  quasi-coherent sheaf vanishes. Combining, \(R^k f_*(\mathcal{C}^p) = 0\) for all
9636	  \(k \geq 1\).
9637	\end{proof}

9676	  \(X\) (so, by separatedness, every intersection \(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\) is
9677	  affine). Then the cohomology sheaves of the relative {\v C}ech complex
9678	  \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathfrak{U}, \mathcal{F})\) of
9679	  Definition~\ref{def:cech_complex} compute the higher direct images: for every
9680	  \(i \geq 0\) there is a canonical isomorphism of \(\mathcal{O}_S\)-modules
9681	  \[

9683	      \;\cong\; R^i f_*\mathcal{F}.

9686	  sections gives \(H^i(X, \mathcal{F}) = \check{H}^i(\mathfrak{U}, \mathcal{F}) =
9687	  H^0(S, R^i f_*\mathcal{F})\) as \(A\)-modules for all \(i\).

9690	  derived-functor higher direct image \(R^i f_*\mathcal{F}\) on the right-hand side
9691	  is the one obtained from injective resolutions, which is available only when the

8431	  \operatorname{sheafify}(H^i(K))\). The supporting bricks are the
8432	  additivity of sheafification, the counit complex isomorphism, and the

8434	  additive homology-preserving functor.

50	`ses_cech_h1`, (3) the dimension-shift `cech_eq_cohomology_of_basis` (01EO) consuming the

52	01EO is the next target. This breaks the circular regress: P3 produces standard-cover Čech
53	vanishing; the bridge lifts it to affine sheaf vanishing without ever using affine vanishing.

56	The augmented Čech complex is a resolution of `F` (ANY `O_X`-module, ANY open cover; qcoh/affineness
57	are needed only by the downstream f_*-acyclicity, not by this exactness). Route: reflect `IsZero(Hᵖ)`

59	helper — `reflects_exact_of_faithful` needs `[Abelian]`, avoid); the homology SHEAF = sheafification of

63	F-valued objectwise analogue), so its sheafification vanishes via `sheafify_kills_locally_zero` + the
